text: When working on a software project with a team, using version control is essential. Git branching allows developers to create separate "feature branches" from the main codebase. This means you can work on a new feature or fix a bug without affecting the stable code. Once your work is finished, you can create a pull request to ask other team members to review your changes. After the review, the changes are integrated into the main branch through merging. This workflow keeps the project organized, reduces conflicts, and ensures that only tested, approved code becomes part of the final product.
